paper.title: An Extremely Massive White Dwarf Escaped From the Hyades Star Cluster

extraction.summary: This paper studies an ultramassive white dwarf WD1 (Gaia EDR3 560883558756079616) that escaped from the Hyades star cluster. WD1 is a cluster runaway, with a reconstructed 3D velocity relative to the Hyades of only ~10 km/s, a radial velocity of ~20 km/s, and a distance of ~167 pc. The paper does not discuss whether WD1 is unbound from the Galaxy (Galactic-unbound), nor does it mention Galactic escape velocity or hypervelocity star (HVS) classification. This object is an ordinary cluster runaway, not an HVS candidate.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Hyades runaway white dwarf candidates: WD1 (Gaia EDR3 560883558756079616, 1.317 Msun), WD2 (Gaia EDR3 3776918275016618112, ~1.1 Msun), WD3 (Gaia EDR3 3072348715677121280, ~1.1 Msun). All three are ultramassive white dwarfs with kinematics consistent with past membership in the Hyades open cluster.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: These are cluster runaways (escaped from the Hyades open cluster), not Galactic-unbound hypervelocity stars. WD1's reconstructed 3D velocity relative to the Hyades cluster is only ~10 km/s, its heliocentric radial velocity is ~20 km/s at ~167 pc. The paper does not discuss escape from the Galaxy or compare velocities to the Galactic escape velocity. No HVS candidates identified.

source_refs.context: Section 3 White Dwarf Escapee Candidates: identifies WD1 as a Hyades cluster runaway with reconstructed 3D velocity ~10 km/s relative to cluster. Paper discusses escape from open cluster only, not Galactic escape.
